title: Maps, Strings, Bytes, Runes, Unicode, and Deterministic Results · Program Defense
summary: An incident summarizer must count labels, normalize text, preserve UTF-8, and publish stable output despite unordered maps. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Unicode-safe deterministic incident summarizer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, update, delete, clear, and query maps while distinguishing missing keys from stored zero values.
Choose comparable key types and explain map reference behavior, iteration instability, mutation rules, and concurrency limits.
Treat strings as immutable byte sequences and convert to or from byte slices with explicit allocation and encoding intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Map Create Access · changed case · Quiz Map Create Access
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Incident labels include "café", "café", "東京", invalid UTF-8, and 5 repeated ASCII keys. Count without confusing missing and zero, report byte and rune evidence, then sort keys for deterministic output.
